category.label: Parameters, Vectors, and Matrices
category.description: AP Precalculus Unit 4 enrichment content covering parametric functions and planar motion, implicitly defined functions and conic sections, vectors and vector-valued functions, and matrix operations and transformations. None of these topics appear on the AP Precalculus exam; yields are capped at 0.15 and reflect value as preparation for calculus or state/local curriculum requirements.
category.yield_rationale: Unit 4 is explicitly not assessed on the AP Precalculus exam (0% exam weight); category yield reflects modest downstream value for calc_ab (parametric motion, vectors, and matrices appear briefly in some calc contexts) but is capped well below 0.15 per spec.

umbrellas.label: Parametric Functions — Introduction
umbrellas.description: Introduces parametric equations as separate x(t) and y(t) component functions that together trace a plane curve; connects parameter elimination to Cartesian form.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Parametric representation is a direct precursor to calculus parametric derivatives and motion problems; highest-yield topic in Unit 4 for calc_ab readiness.

in_depth.label: Parametric equations as component functions
in_depth.description: Tests whether a student can identify x(t) and y(t) as functions of a shared parameter t and interpret the parameter as an input that generates ordered pairs tracing a curve.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Core definitional skill; gate for all subsequent parametric work and calc_ab parametric derivatives.

in_depth.label: Generating a table of (x, y) pairs from parametric equations
in_depth.description: Tests the ability to substitute specific t-values into x(t) and y(t) to produce a table of ordered pairs and identify the direction of traversal.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Procedural skill; supports graphing and orientation but limited standalone exam relevance.

in_depth.label: Eliminating the parameter when x(t) is linear
in_depth.description: Tests solving x(t) = at + b for t, then substituting into y(t) to obtain a Cartesian equation y = f(x), identifying any domain restrictions inherited from the parameter range.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Parameter elimination is the primary algebraic technique of 4.1 and re-appears in conic parametrization (4.7).

in_depth.label: Eliminating the parameter using a Pythagorean identity
in_depth.description: Tests using sin²(t) + cos²(t) = 1 to eliminate t from x = r·cos(t), y = r·sin(t)-style equations, obtaining the Cartesian form of a circle or ellipse.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Connects parametric and trig content; sets up parametric circles (4.4) and conics (4.7).

in_depth.label: Identifying the orientation (direction) of a parametric curve
in_depth.description: Tests reading or sketching a parametric curve and correctly marking arrows that show the direction of increasing t, distinguishing between two Cartesian curves with opposite orientations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Orientation is a representational concept unique to parametric form; lower yield as a standalone question but conceptually important.

umbrellas.label: Parametric Functions and Planar Motion
umbrellas.description: Uses parametric equations to model an object's position in the plane over time; interprets x(t) and y(t) as horizontal and vertical position components, and determines direction and speed.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Position-velocity-speed interpretation of parametric motion is directly prerequisite for AP Calc AB parametric derivatives and motion problems; strongest calc_ab connection in Unit 4.

in_depth.label: Reading position at a given time from parametric motion equations
in_depth.description: Tests substituting a specific t-value into x(t) and y(t) to find the (x, y) location of a moving object, and interpreting the result in a real-world context (e.g., height, horizontal distance).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Direct application of parametric evaluation in a motion context; connects to AP Calc AB motion problems.

in_depth.label: Determining direction of travel from signs of x'(t) and y'(t)
in_depth.description: Tests identifying whether an object is moving left/right and up/down at a given instant by interpreting the signs of the average rates of change (or slope of the parametric curve) over a small interval.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign-of-velocity reasoning is a calc_ab conceptual prerequisite; moderate standalone yield in a precalc context.

in_depth.label: Computing speed as the magnitude of the velocity vector
in_depth.description: Tests computing instantaneous or average speed as √((Δx/Δt)² + (Δy/Δt)²), connecting the Pythagorean theorem to the distance formula in a parametric motion context.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Speed formula bridges parametric motion and vector magnitude; useful setup for calc_ab.

in_depth.label: Distinguishing path (Cartesian curve) from trajectory (parametric curve with time)
in_depth.description: Tests identifying that two parametric equations can trace the same Cartesian path at different speeds or in different directions, so the parameter carries information beyond the shape.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Conceptual distinction; appears in reasoning questions but is lower-yield as an isolated skill.

umbrellas.label: Rates of Change in Parametric Functions
umbrellas.description: Analyzes average rates of change of x(t) and y(t) separately, and interprets the ratio Δy/Δx (slope of secant) using the parametric components.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Rate-of-change reasoning in a parametric context previews the dy/dx = (dy/dt)/(dx/dt) formula in calc_ab; moderate downstream value.

in_depth.label: Calculating average rate of change for a single parametric component
in_depth.description: Tests computing Δx/Δt or Δy/Δt over an interval [t₁, t₂] from a table or formula and interpreting the sign and magnitude in context.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Standard average-rate-of-change skill applied to parametric components; moderate yield.

in_depth.label: Finding the slope of a parametric curve as (Δy/Δt) ÷ (Δx/Δt)
in_depth.description: Tests computing the approximate slope of a parametric curve at a parameter value using the ratio of component rates of change, connecting to the tangent-line concept.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Direct preview of parametric derivative formula in AP Calc AB; best yield in this umbrella.

in_depth.label: Interpreting the meaning of Δx/Δt and Δy/Δt in a real-world motion problem
in_depth.description: Tests translating numerical values of Δx/Δt and Δy/Δt into contextual language (e.g., 'the object moves 3 meters per second horizontally') given units and scenario description.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Contextual interpretation is valued in communication practice but is low-yield as an isolated problem type.

in_depth.label: Determining intervals where x(t) or y(t) is increasing or decreasing
in_depth.description: Tests reading a table or graph to identify t-intervals where a parametric component is increasing (positive rate of change) or decreasing (negative rate of change).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Increasing/decreasing analysis using a table is a transferable skill but niche in the parametric context.

umbrellas.label: Parametrically Defined Circles and Lines
umbrellas.description: Writes and interprets standard parametric forms for circles (x = h + r·cos t, y = k + r·sin t) and lines (x = x₀ + at, y = y₀ + bt), connecting each parameter to geometric features.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Parametric form of circles and lines is a clean, bounded topic with direct connections to vectors (4.8) and conics (4.6–4.7); yield is limited by non-exam status.

in_depth.label: Writing parametric equations for a circle from center and radius
in_depth.description: Tests producing x(t) = h + r·cos(t), y(t) = k + r·sin(t) given center (h, k) and radius r, and identifying the parameter range for one full traversal.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Core parametric form for circles; links to trig unit and elimination-via-Pythagorean-identity skill.

in_depth.label: Writing parametric equations for a line through a point with a direction vector
in_depth.description: Tests producing x(t) = x₀ + at, y(t) = y₀ + bt given a point on the line and a direction vector ⟨a, b⟩, and converting to slope-intercept form by eliminating t.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Connects parametric lines to vectors (4.8) and to standard Cartesian line equations; useful bridge topic.

in_depth.label: Identifying center, radius, and orientation from parametric circle equations
in_depth.description: Tests reading h, k, r, and traversal direction (clockwise vs. counterclockwise) from a given parametric form x(t) = h + r·cos(t), y(t) = k + r·sin(t).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Feature-identification question type; lower yield than construction but tests representation literacy.

in_depth.label: Restricting the parameter range to produce a line segment
in_depth.description: Tests determining the t-values corresponding to two given endpoints and expressing the line segment as a parametric function with a restricted domain.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Domain restriction of parametric functions is a niche but clean problem type; low standalone yield.

umbrellas.label: Implicitly Defined Functions
umbrellas.description: Analyzes relations defined by an equation F(x, y) = 0 that cannot be written as a single explicit function; identifies when an implicit relation can be restricted to a function and determines domain/range from the equation.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Implicit functions preview implicit differentiation in calc_ab; precalc treatment is conceptual/graphical and low-yield on its own.

in_depth.label: Determining whether an implicit equation defines y as a function of x
in_depth.description: Tests applying the vertical-line test or solving for y to check whether a given implicit relation F(x, y) = 0 defines one or more functions, identifying cases where it does not (e.g., full circle).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Function/relation distinction is foundational and tested across units; highest yield in this umbrella.

in_depth.label: Restricting domain or range to extract a function from an implicit relation
in_depth.description: Tests identifying a restriction (e.g., y ≥ 0 for the upper semicircle of x² + y² = r²) that makes the relation a function and writing the resulting explicit function.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Connects to inverse-function restricted-domain reasoning; moderate downstream value.

in_depth.label: Reading key features of an implicitly defined curve from its graph
in_depth.description: Tests identifying x- and y-intercepts, symmetry, and approximate domain/range of an implicitly defined curve displayed graphically, without solving the equation explicitly.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Graphical reading skill with low problem-type specificity; borderline useful for calc_ab intuition.

in_depth.label: Solving an implicit equation for y to obtain explicit function branches
in_depth.description: Tests algebraically isolating y from an implicit equation such as x² + y² = r² or 4x² + 9y² = 36 to obtain ±√(…) and interpreting each branch as a separate function.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Algebraic manipulation skill linking implicit equations to their explicit function representations; moderate yield.

umbrellas.label: Conic Sections
umbrellas.description: Identifies and analyzes parabolas, ellipses, and hyperbolas in standard form; connects equation form to geometric features (vertices, foci, axes, asymptotes).
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Conics appear in many state curricula and in some calc_ab contexts (related rates with ellipses); the structured equation-feature connection makes for clean, well-scoped problems.

in_depth.label: Identifying vertex, axis of symmetry, and direction of opening of a parabola
in_depth.description: Tests reading a, h, k from y = a(x − h)² + k (or x = a(y − k)² + h) to state the vertex, axis, and whether the parabola opens up/down/left/right.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Parabola analysis bridges F5 (polynomial function behavior) and conics; most accessible conic and highest yield here.

in_depth.label: Identifying center, semi-axes, and orientation of an ellipse from standard form
in_depth.description: Tests extracting h, k, a, b from (x − h)²/a² + (y − k)²/b² = 1 to determine the center, lengths of the semi-major and semi-minor axes, and which axis is major.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Ellipse feature-reading is a clean, well-defined question type; moderate yield given non-exam status.

in_depth.label: Writing the asymptotes of a hyperbola from its standard equation
in_depth.description: Tests determining asymptote equations y − k = ±(b/a)(x − h) for horizontal hyperbolas and y − k = ±(a/b)(x − h) for vertical ones using the center and a, b values.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Asymptote computation is the most distinctive hyperbola skill and a well-scoped problem type.

in_depth.label: Classifying a conic section from its general second-degree equation
in_depth.description: Tests deciding whether Ax² + Bxy + Cy² + Dx + Ey + F = 0 represents a parabola, ellipse, or hyperbola using the discriminant B² − 4AC or by completing the square to reach standard form.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Classification is a higher-order skill requiring multiple algebraic steps; moderate yield in enrichment context.

in_depth.label: Completing the square to rewrite a conic in standard form
in_depth.description: Tests grouping x- and y-terms, completing the square for each variable, and rewriting the equation in (x − h)²/a² + (y − k)²/b² = 1 (or analogous) standard form to read off center and radii.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Completing the square is a high-value algebraic technique used in conics, quadratic functions, and calc_ab optimization; strongest downstream yield in this umbrella.

umbrellas.label: Parametrization of Implicit and Conic Curves
umbrellas.description: Converts implicitly defined curves and conic sections to parametric form; reverses the elimination process to produce x(t) and y(t) that trace the curve.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Parametrization of conics synthesizes topics 4.4–4.6 and is useful for calc_ab arc length and polar-to-parametric connections; low standalone yield.

in_depth.label: Writing parametric equations for an ellipse using cosine and sine
in_depth.description: Tests producing x(t) = h + a·cos(t), y(t) = k + b·sin(t) from an ellipse equation (x−h)²/a² + (y−k)²/b² = 1 and verifying by eliminating t.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Clean, testable skill that links trigonometric parametrization to conic geometry.

in_depth.label: Verifying that a parametrization satisfies an implicit equation
in_depth.description: Tests substituting given x(t) and y(t) into an implicit equation F(x, y) = 0 and simplifying using identities to confirm the parametrization is valid.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Algebraic verification skill; useful for rigor but low practical yield in a precalc enrichment context.

in_depth.label: Parametrizing a hyperbola using sec and tan
in_depth.description: Tests writing x(t) = h + a·sec(t), y(t) = k + b·tan(t) for a horizontal hyperbola and connecting the identity sec²(t) − tan²(t) = 1 to the standard hyperbola form.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Niche skill combining reciprocal trig with conics; lowest yield in the umbrella but covers a distinct parametrization technique.

in_depth.label: Converting a piecewise or line parametrization to match an implicit curve restriction
in_depth.description: Tests choosing appropriate domain restrictions on t so that the parametrization traces only a specified arc or portion of an implicitly defined curve (e.g., upper half only).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Domain restriction in parametric form is a bounded, solvable problem type; connects to restricted-function concepts.

umbrellas.label: Vectors — Definition and Operations
umbrellas.description: Defines vectors as quantities with magnitude and direction; performs vector addition and subtraction and scalar multiplication both algebraically (component form ⟨a, b⟩) and graphically (tip-to-tail).
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Vectors are directly used in AP Calc AB (velocity/displacement as vectors) and in many STEM curricula; highest category-wide yield after parametric motion.

in_depth.label: Writing a vector in component form given initial and terminal points
in_depth.description: Tests computing ⟨x₂ − x₁, y₂ − y₁⟩ from a directed segment P₁P₂ and interpreting the components as horizontal and vertical displacements.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Component form is the fundamental algebraic representation of vectors; gate for all vector operations.

in_depth.label: Computing the magnitude of a vector
in_depth.description: Tests applying |v| = √(a² + b²) to a vector ⟨a, b⟩ in component form, connecting it to the Pythagorean theorem and the distance formula.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Magnitude is used in speed (4.2), unit vectors, and dot product; central vector skill.

in_depth.label: Adding and subtracting vectors algebraically and graphically
in_depth.description: Tests computing ⟨a₁ + a₂, b₁ + b₂⟩ for vector sum and ⟨a₁ − a₂, b₁ − b₂⟩ for difference, and connecting the algebraic result to the tip-to-tail and parallelogram graphic representations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Vector addition is the central operation in displacement and force contexts; appears in both numeric and graphical problem types.

in_depth.label: Multiplying a vector by a scalar and interpreting the result
in_depth.description: Tests computing k·⟨a, b⟩ = ⟨ka, kb⟩ for scalar k, describing the effect on direction (same/reversed) and magnitude (|k|·|v|), and identifying unit vectors.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Scalar multiplication is prerequisite for unit vectors and linear combinations; moderate yield.

in_depth.label: Finding the direction angle of a vector from its component form
in_depth.description: Tests computing θ = arctan(b/a) with quadrant adjustment to find the angle a vector makes with the positive x-axis, connecting to inverse trig (3.9) and polar coordinates (3.13).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Direction angle bridges vectors and polar/trig content; useful synthesis but limited standalone yield in enrichment context.

in_depth.label: Computing the unit vector in the direction of a given vector
in_depth.description: Tests dividing a vector by its magnitude to obtain a vector of length 1, and using unit vectors to write a vector as a magnitude-direction product.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Unit vectors are used in physics and calculus contexts; moderate yield as a distinct skill.

umbrellas.label: Vector-Valued Functions
umbrellas.description: Expresses planar motion using a position vector r(t) = ⟨x(t), y(t)⟩; evaluates vector-valued functions and connects them to the parametric function representation of a path.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Vector-valued functions unify parametric motion (4.2) and vectors (4.8) into the notation used throughout AP Calc BC and some calc_ab motion problems; moderate downstream yield.

in_depth.label: Evaluating a vector-valued function at a specific t
in_depth.description: Tests substituting a value of t into r(t) = ⟨x(t), y(t)⟩ to obtain a position vector, and interpreting the result as a point in the plane.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Basic evaluation skill; gate for motion analysis using vector notation.

in_depth.label: Connecting vector-valued function notation to component parametric equations
in_depth.description: Tests rewriting r(t) = ⟨x(t), y(t)⟩ as the parametric pair x = x(t), y = y(t) and vice versa, confirming that both describe the same curve with the same orientation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Representation translation between two equivalent notations for the same curve; useful for calc_ab motion problems.

in_depth.label: Computing the displacement vector over a time interval
in_depth.description: Tests finding r(t₂) − r(t₁) = ⟨x(t₂) − x(t₁), y(t₂) − y(t₁)⟩ and interpreting its magnitude as the straight-line distance between two positions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Displacement as a vector subtraction previews integral-based displacement in calc_ab; moderate yield.

in_depth.label: Identifying the Cartesian path traced by a vector-valued function
in_depth.description: Tests eliminating the parameter from r(t) = ⟨x(t), y(t)⟩ to find the Cartesian equation of the path, and determining any domain restrictions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Path identification combines parametric elimination with vector notation; synthesis skill.

umbrellas.label: Matrix Operations
umbrellas.description: Defines matrices and performs addition, subtraction, and multiplication of matrices; identifies when operations are defined based on matrix dimensions.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Matrix arithmetic is the foundation for all subsequent matrix topics (4.11–4.14); modest yield as operations appear in linear algebra and statistics downstream but not in AP Calc AB.

in_depth.label: Reading matrix dimensions and entries using row-column notation
in_depth.description: Tests identifying the number of rows and columns (m × n) of a matrix and accessing entry aᵢⱼ, correctly applying the row-first, column-second convention.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Definitional notation skill; prerequisite for multiplication dimension-check.

in_depth.label: Adding and subtracting matrices of the same dimensions
in_depth.description: Tests performing entry-wise addition/subtraction on matrices of equal dimensions and recognizing that the operation is undefined for matrices of different sizes.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Straightforward entry-wise operation; limited standalone yield but necessary foundation.

in_depth.label: Multiplying a matrix by a scalar
in_depth.description: Tests multiplying every entry of a matrix by a scalar k and interpreting the result, including negative and fractional scalars.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Analogous to vector scalar multiplication; low standalone yield.

in_depth.label: Multiplying two 2×2 matrices using the dot-product rule
in_depth.description: Tests computing the 2×2 product AB by taking dot products of rows of A with columns of B, verifying the dimension condition (columns of A = rows of B), and noting non-commutativity.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Matrix multiplication is the most conceptually dense matrix operation and directly required for transformations (4.12) and matrix-as-function (4.13); highest yield in this umbrella.

in_depth.label: Determining when matrix multiplication is defined and finding the product dimensions
in_depth.description: Tests applying the m×n · n×p → m×p rule to decide whether two matrices can be multiplied and what size the product will be.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Dimension compatibility is a clean, bounded rule with frequent application in multi-step matrix problems.

umbrellas.label: Inverse and Determinant of a Matrix
umbrellas.description: Computes the determinant of a 2×2 matrix, determines whether the matrix is invertible (det ≠ 0), and finds the inverse matrix using the det-based formula.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Determinant and inverse are required for solving matrix equations (AX = B → X = A⁻¹B); modest downstream yield for systems of equations and linear transformations.

in_depth.label: Computing the determinant of a 2×2 matrix
in_depth.description: Tests applying det([[a,b],[c,d]]) = ad − bc to a given 2×2 matrix, including cases with negative entries, fractions, and variable entries.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Determinant formula is the single most frequently needed matrix computation; highest yield in umbrella.

in_depth.label: Determining invertibility from the determinant
in_depth.description: Tests identifying that a matrix is invertible if and only if its determinant is non-zero, and stating what a zero determinant implies geometrically (collapsed transformation).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Invertibility condition connects determinant to solvability of matrix equations; moderate yield.

in_depth.label: Finding the inverse of a 2×2 invertible matrix
in_depth.description: Tests applying A⁻¹ = (1/det(A))·[[d,−b],[−c,a]] to a given 2×2 matrix, checking the result via AA⁻¹ = I.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Inverse formula is a clean, bounded algorithmic skill; connects to matrix equations and transformations.

in_depth.label: Solving a 2×2 matrix equation AX = B using the inverse
in_depth.description: Tests computing X = A⁻¹B when A is invertible, connecting the matrix equation to a system of two linear equations and verifying the solution.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Matrix-equation solving is the applied payoff of learning inverses; moderate yield and clean problem type.

umbrellas.label: Linear Transformations and Matrices
umbrellas.description: Represents geometric transformations of the plane (reflections, rotations, dilations, shears) as left-multiplication by a 2×2 matrix, and computes the image of a point or figure under the transformation.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Linear transformations connect matrix multiplication to geometry; moderate yield for courses covering linear algebra foundations and for understanding function transformations in a coordinate sense.

in_depth.label: Computing the image of a point under a matrix transformation
in_depth.description: Tests multiplying a 2×2 transformation matrix A by a column vector representing a point to find its image, interpreting the result as a new coordinate pair.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Core application of matrix multiplication in a geometric setting; highest yield in umbrella.

in_depth.label: Identifying standard transformation matrices for reflection and rotation
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing or producing the matrix for rotation by 90°/180°, reflection across x-axis, y-axis, or y = x, and dilation by factor k.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Standard matrices are a memorizable but meaningful set of examples; moderate yield.

in_depth.label: Composing two linear transformations using matrix multiplication
in_depth.description: Tests computing the composition of two transformations as the matrix product AB and applying the composite matrix to a point, noting that AB ≠ BA in general.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Composition of transformations previews function composition in a geometric context; connects to 2.7.

in_depth.label: Interpreting the determinant as a scaling factor for area under a transformation
in_depth.description: Tests using |det(A)| to determine how much a linear transformation scales areas, and identifying that det = 1 preserves area while det = 0 collapses the plane.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Geometric interpretation of determinant is a rich concept but low-yield as a standalone precalc question type.

umbrellas.label: Matrices as Functions
umbrellas.description: Interprets a 2×2 matrix as a linear function T: ℝ² → ℝ² that maps input vectors to output vectors; analyzes properties such as linearity, the identity transformation, and the zero transformation.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Matrix-as-function is a conceptual bridge to linear algebra; lowest yield in Unit 4 as a standalone topic with limited AP Calc AB application.

in_depth.label: Verifying that a matrix transformation satisfies the linearity properties
in_depth.description: Tests confirming T(u + v) = T(u) + T(v) and T(cu) = cT(u) for specific vectors by direct computation, connecting the algebraic definition of linearity to the matrix-multiplication structure.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Abstract linearity verification; enrichment-level concept with minimal practical standalone yield.

in_depth.label: Identifying the identity and zero matrix transformations
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing the 2×2 identity matrix I = [[1,0],[0,1]] and zero matrix as the identity transformation (every vector maps to itself) and the zero transformation (every vector maps to 0) respectively.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Definitional knowledge of special matrices; low standalone yield but conceptually necessary.

in_depth.label: Describing the domain and codomain of a matrix transformation
in_depth.description: Tests identifying that an m×n matrix defines a function from ℝⁿ to ℝᵐ, and determining whether a given column vector has the correct dimension to be a valid input.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Dimension-aware function thinking is useful for linear algebra readiness; very low AP precalc yield.

in_depth.label: Contrasting matrix (linear) functions with general nonlinear functions
in_depth.description: Tests identifying features that distinguish linear transformations (no constant term, maps zero to zero) from general function models (exponential, quadratic, sinusoidal) studied in Units 1–3.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Conceptual comparison across function families; supports unit-coherence but low problem-type yield.

umbrellas.label: Matrices Modeling Contexts
umbrellas.description: Applies matrices to model real-world situations such as transition matrices (Markov chains), network adjacency matrices, and population or economic models; interprets matrix powers and products contextually.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Contextual matrix applications are the most applied topic in Unit 4; modest yield reflects non-exam status but these problems are engaging and showcase matrix utility.

in_depth.label: Setting up a matrix to represent a two-state transition or network
in_depth.description: Tests reading a verbal description of transitions between two states (or connections in a small network) and constructing the corresponding 2×2 matrix with correct entry placement.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Matrix setup from context is a clean verbal-to-symbolic skill; highest yield in this umbrella.

in_depth.label: Using matrix multiplication to find the next-state vector
in_depth.description: Tests multiplying a transition matrix by a state vector to obtain the distribution after one step, and interpreting each entry of the result as a count or probability.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Next-state computation is the core application of transition matrices; clean problem type.

in_depth.label: Interpreting the (i, j) entry of a matrix product in context
in_depth.description: Tests reading a matrix product AB and explaining what the entry in row i, column j represents in the real-world scenario encoded by A and B (e.g., two-step path counts in a network).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Entry interpretation is a reasoning skill; lower yield as a standalone question but supports conceptual understanding.

in_depth.label: Identifying or approximating a steady-state vector by iterating the transition matrix
in_depth.description: Tests applying a transition matrix repeatedly to an initial state vector and observing convergence to a limiting distribution, recognizing it as a fixed point satisfying Av = v.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Steady-state concept is enrichment-level; interesting for motivated students but low AP exam relevance.
